items.title: When working more hours might mean you lose your home

children.content: Fremantle resident Trish Owen could lose the tenancy on her government-provided home if she works more hours — and a new study suggests many people are in the same situation.

children.content: Jesse Bruzzese's family say the 35-year-old solo traveller has not been in touch with them since arriving in Mexico at the beginning of June.

items.title: Photos show room where police allege Thai teenager was last seen

children.content: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman, who moved to Thailand from WA.

articles.title: Australian 'lived normal life' before allegedly dumping Thai teen's body

children.content: Australian Simon Peter Carman "lived his normal life", going out, washing his clothes and talking to neighbours after allegedly murdering a teenage girl and stuffing her body inside a suitcase in Thailand, police say.

items.title: Paediatrician accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges

children.content: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

items.title: Accounting firms could be split in wake of KPMG audit scandal

children.content: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.

items.title: Bronze tugboat propellers worth $2m stolen in WA's north

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

items.title: One of the last states allowing 'conversion practices' is on path to ban them

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.
